title: Lafarge Africa Shareholders Approves N1.90kobo Dividend … Gbenga Oyebode Takes Over As Chairman - FinancialEDGE
description: Shareholders of Lafarge Africa Plc, have approved the proposed dividend of N1.90 kobo for the financial year ended December 31, 2023. html:
Shareholders of Lafarge Africa Plc, have approved the proposed dividend of N1.90 kobo for the financial year ended December 31, 2023. Speaking at the company 65th Annual General Meeting (AGM) in Lagos, the chairman of the company Mr Adebode Adefoiye explained that the dividend is reflective of the company’s prudent capital allocation strategy.

Just as Mr Gbenga Oyebode will take over from  Mr Adebode Adefioye, as the chairman of Lafarge Africa Plc.

Adefioye noted that the dividend “Underscores our commitment to delivering sustainable value to our esteemed shareholders even in these challenging times”.

The dividend which is payable from the company pioneer reserve will be paid today 26th April 2024, to shareholders whose name appears on the register of members as at the close of business on Thursday 28th march 2024.

While responding to questions from shareholders, the Group Managing Director, Mr Lolu Alade Akinyemi, said Lafarge Africa remains positive about the prospect of the Nigerian infrastructure and construction sector, despite prevailing economic challenges.

“Our unwavering commitment to sustainability and innovation will drive our strategic initiatives in the year 2024”.

Adding that “We are poised to launch transformative projects, including the commissioning of the Evacuation road in calabar, the alternative fuel and raw material project in Mfammosng plant and the Captive power plant in Ashaka “.

He pointed out that the company’s relentless pursuit of product innovation will enable them tMo address industry specific challenges and solidify their position as a preferred provider of building solutions.
